text: Some species punch above their weight — remove them and ecosystems unravel. These are called keystone species, vital to their habitats’ health. Take the sea otter; without it, urchin populations explode and kelp forests vanish. Or consider wolves in Yellowstone. Their presence balances elk populations, promoting plant diversity. Removing keystone species can lead to domino effects, collapsing entire ecosystems. But it’s not just about animals. Plants can be keystone too. Think of the mighty fig tree, supporting countless species. Curious about more ecological wonders? Hit that follow button!
